steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The interior angles of a triangle add to $180^\circ$. Here the three angle measures are $47^\circ$, $90^\circ$, and $$(\frac{k}{2})^\circ$$
$$47+90+\frac{k}{2}=180$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content: Combine the known angle measures, then isolate $k$. $$137+\frac{k}{2}=180$$ $$\frac{k}{2}=43$$ $$k=86$$ The answer is C.
